tools.name: codespell
tools.scope: All text files (excludes tests/models/fixtures, build/, etc.)

tools.name: clang-format
tools.scope: C++ and CUDA files (excludes third_party, certain GGUF files)

tools.name: pre-commit
tools.scope: All configured hooks (yapf, ruff, mypy, isort, codespell, clang-format, etc.)

linting.notes: Linting is enforced via pre-commit hooks. CI runs pre-commit with --hook-stage manual to run all hooks including mypy for multiple Python versions (3.9-3.12). Ruff found 472 lint violations in current codebase (mostly in third_party code).

setup_required: Requires CUDA toolkit and GPU for most tests
env_vars: VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=XFORMERS or FLASH_ATTN (for attention backend tests)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest fixtures for model loading, fixtures defined in conftest.py
testing.notes: Tests are heavily GPU-dependent. Most tests require NVIDIA CUDA GPUs. Test suite is organized by Buildkite pipeline (not GitHub Actions). Tests use pytest markers: skip_global_cleanup, core_model, cpu_model, quant_model, distributed, skip_v1, optional. Tests can be sharded using pytest-shard: pytest --shard-id=X --num-shards=Y. Many tests require specific models to be downloaded from HuggingFace or S3.

advisory_checks.name: Buildkite tests
advisory_checks.notes: Buildkite tests are the authoritative test suite but run on specialized infrastructure with GPUs

ci.notes: GitHub Actions only runs pre-commit checks and Helm chart validation. The real test suite runs on Buildkite with GPU infrastructure. Buildkite pipeline includes ~60 test jobs covering: fast checks (basic correctness, core, entrypoints), distributed tests (2-4 GPUs), model tests, LoRA tests, kernel tests, quantization tests, etc. Most tests require L4 or A100 GPUs. Some tests are marked 'optional' and only run on nightly builds or when manually triggered.

build.system: setuptools with CMake/ninja for C++/CUDA extensions
build.notes: Build requires CUDA toolkit (12.4.1 in Dockerfile), CMake >=3.26, ninja, GCC 10+, and PyTorch. Build is complex with C++/CUDA kernels. For development without GPU, can use python-only mode. Full build creates wheels with --build-option flags. Different requirements files for different platforms: requirements-cuda.txt, requirements-rocm.txt, requirements-cpu.txt, requirements-tpu.txt, requirements-hpu.txt, requirements-neuron.txt, requirements-openvino.txt, requirements-xpu.txt.

conventions.import_style: Absolute imports, grouped and sorted by isort. Third-party code in vllm/third_party/ is excluded from linting.
conventions.notes: Code requires SPDX license headers (checked by pre-commit hook). All commits must be signed off (enforced by pre-commit hook). No spaces allowed in filenames. C++/CUDA code formatted with clang-format. Shell scripts checked with shellcheck. PNG files checked for excalidraw exports. Markdown files checked with pymarkdown.

setup_commands: pip install ruff yapf isort mypy codespell
pip install pytest==8.3.3 pytest-asyncio pytest-forked pytest-mock

lint_commands.notes: Found 472 lint errors (mostly in third_party code). Returns exit code 0 despite errors, which is expected behavior.
Not fully validated - requires all hook dependencies and potentially CUDA for some checks

test_commands.notes: Cannot collect tests without full vllm installation, which requires CUDA build
Cannot run tests - requires vllm to be built and installed with CUDA support

container_recipe.notes: Full test validation requires CUDA GPU and building vllm from source with CUDA extensions. Linting can be validated without GPU. The project is designed for GPU/CUDA environments and most functionality requires NVIDIA GPUs (L4, A100, etc.). CPU-only testing is very limited.

results.notes: Test collection requires full vllm dependencies (numpy, PIL, transformers, etc.) and ultimately requires vllm to be built with CUDA

validation.summary: Lint tools validated OK (ruff found 472 violations). Tests require CUDA/GPU infrastructure - cannot validate in standard container.

gaps: Full test suite requires NVIDIA GPU (L4 or A100) and CUDA toolkit - cannot be validated in CPU-only container
Building vllm from source requires CUDA 12.4.1, CMake, ninja, and is a complex multi-stage process
Many tests require downloading large models from HuggingFace (meta-llama/Llama models, etc.)
Buildkite CI pipeline is the authoritative test suite but is not publicly accessible
No simple way to run a subset of tests without GPU - most tests import vllm which requires CUDA build
Pre-commit hooks include mypy checks that run in CI for Python 3.9-3.12 but require environment setup
Some tests require multi-GPU setup (2-4 GPUs) or even multi-node distributed setup
Documentation tests require sphinx and building docs
Some tests are marked 'optional' and only run in nightly builds or require manual triggering
